rows.summary: Identical substantive content; only minor punctuation difference (period added after 'financing' in NEW) and page number shift.

The franchisor increased the maximum amount financed from 50% to 80%, reduced the minimum down payment from 50% to 20%, and extended the repayment period from 24 to 36 months — all while retaining the same 12% interest rate and personal guarantee requirement. Language on note assignment was expanded to explicitly permit sale/assignment to affiliates or third parties with immunity.

newEvidence.quote: Down Payment                        Minimum of 20%*

NEW Item 10 states unequivocally that neither the franchisor nor any agent or affiliate offers direct or indirect financing, guarantees obligations, or participates in financing arrangements — a complete removal of all prior financing-related disclosures.

The franchisor replaced the prior 'New Development Projects' incentive (3%/4% franchise fee reduction) with a multi-tiered, time-bound franchise fee schedule (3%/3.5%/4%/5.5%), added a $10,000 application fee discount and $5,000/guestroom key money incentive, and updated eligibility windows to March 31, 2026–March 30, 2027. The core mezzanine/contingent guaranty financing offer remains but is now explicitly tied to 'Aloft by Marriott Hotels' and includes new language about 'credit support' — though no new direct financing is introduced.

Year of Operation             Percentage  of  Gross Room Sales

rows.summary: The 2014 FDD explicitly states that affiliate SMAC offers financing for the initial and conversion franchise fees, with detailed terms including interest rates, amounts financed, and security requirements. The 2024 FDD completely removes that offer and affirmatively states that neither the franchisor nor its affiliates offer *any* direct or indirect financing — and further clarifies they do not guarantee notes, leases, or other obligations, nor have arrangements with third-party lenders.

oldEvidence.quote: We do not offer direct fmancing  Our affiliate. The ServiceMaster Acceptance Company Limited Partnership ("SMAC") (see Item  1 disclosure), does offer financing for the Initial Franchise Fee
With your approved credit, SMAC will finance up to 80% of the initial franchise fee up to a seven-year penod
SMAC charges annual interest on the outstanding principal balance at the then current pnme interest rate... plus 5% for new franchisees (subject to a floor of 8 25%)

newEvidence.quote: We and our affiliates do not offer direct or indirect financing arrangements for any purpose in establishing or operating your Franchised Business.
We and our affiliates do not guarantee your promissory note, lease, or any other obligation you may make to others.
we do not have any arrangements with such lenders and do not receive any benefits from such lenders if you obtain financing from them.

rows.summary: Identical substantive disclosure: both versions state the franchisor does not offer direct or indirect financing and does not guarantee franchisee notes, leases, or other obligations.

The 2017 FDD explicitly offered franchisor/affiliate financing on a case-by-case basis, including interest rate (Prime + 3%), term (12–18 months), promissory note requirements, personal guarantee, and real-world examples of six financed franchisees. The 2026 FDD states flatly that neither the franchisor nor its affiliate offers any direct or indirect financing — a complete removal of the financing offer.

oldEvidence.quote: Typically, neither we nor our Affiliate offer financing of the initial franchise fee, or, if applicable, the Going Concern Payment, but we may do so periodically, on a case-by-case basis, with terms and conditions designed to meet your and our particular needs, capabilities and financial requirements.

As disclosed in Item 5 of this disclosure document, although neither we nor our Affiliate typically do so, in 2016 and 2017 (up to the date of this disclosure document), our Affiliate agreed to provide financing to six AmSpirit franchisees for the purchase of an AmSpirit franchise.

The promissory note contains certain material terms of which you should be aware. These include the following: (1) You have the right to prepay the note without penalty, (Installment Promissory Note, Paragraph 2).

newEvidence.quote: Neither we nor our Affiliate offer direct or indirect financing to you.

rows.summary: OLD states no direct financing and no guarantee of obligations; NEW introduces a direct franchisor financing offer up to $70,000 at 10% interest for 60 months, requiring a promissory note and personal guarantee, with detailed default and acceleration terms.

oldEvidence.quote: We do not offer direct financing We do not guarantee any note, lease or other of your obligations

newEvidence.quote: We may, in our sole discretion, provide financing to you if you are a qualified franchisee based on your credit-worthiness and our current financing policies.

If we agree to provide financing, we will loan you an amount up to $70,000 based on approximately 20% of the actual amount it will cost you to establish your Facility excluding the Initial Franchise Fee (“Financed Amount”).
You and your owners will personally guaranty the payment of the Promissory Note.

rows.summary: Identical text in both years: explicit statement that franchisor offers no direct or indirect financing and provides no guarantee of franchisee notes, leases, or obligations.

The franchisor eliminated its prior direct financing offer for initial franchise fees, equipment, and sales/marketing fees (including veteran-specific 0% financing), replacing it with a narrower, conditional offer limited to 'qualifying existing franchisees' for up to 80% of the franchise fee only; interest shifted from fixed 10%/15% to variable (prime + 4%), maturity capped at 24 months (down from 36), security interest removed, personal guarantee requirement narrowed to only those already required under the Guarantee and Assumption form, and waiver of legal rights (jury trial, presentment, etc.) was eliminated.

oldEvidence.quote: We may finance a portion of the Initial Franchise Fee for qualified franchisees. We also may finance a portion of the Sales and Marketing Fee if we offer you and you accept Additional Accounts. Financing also is available for equipment leases.

We may finance between $2,000 and $7,500 of your Initial Franchise Fee depending on the Initial Plan you buy. We may finance up to 75% of the purchase price for certain equipment you buy from us for 12 to 36 months.
Under this Guaranty, they guarantee that you will perform all your obligations under the Promissory Note.

newEvidence.quote: Under limited and special circumstances, we may allow for a partial deferral or make optional financing available to qualifying existing franchisees.
The effective annual interest rate will be four percentage points above the prime interest rate on the effective date of the Franchise Agreement.
You will not waive any defenses or other legal rights under the Promissory Note.

rows.summary: The franchisor introduced a direct deferred-payment financing option for the Initial Franchise Fee — allowing installment payments deducted from franchisee revenue — which did not exist in the 2014 FDD. The 2014 FDD explicitly stated 'We do not offer financing for the Initial Franchise Fee'; the 2025 FDD replaces that with an affirmative, structured deferred-payment plan. Other changes include reduced affiliate lease commission (7½% → 2%), addition of mandatory equipment insurance, and introduction of an automatic 30-day credit line tied to billing cycles.

oldEvidence.quote: We do not offer financing for the Initial Franchise Fee.

newEvidence.quote: We do not offer any traditional financing for the Initial Franchise Fee. However, instead of paying the entire Initial Franchise Fee when you sign the Franchise Agreement, we will allow you to elect to pay a portion of the Initial Franchise Fee over time.

oldEvidence.quote: We do not offer direct or indirect financing. We do not guarantee your note, lease or obligation.

newEvidence.quote: in limited circumstances and in our sole discretion, we or one of our affiliates may provide financing in connection with your purchase of a corporate-owned restaurant sold on an “as-is” basis
If you finance any portion of the purchase price of the corporate-owned restaurant through one of our affiliates, you will also enter into a “Promissory Note and Security Agreement” and a “Guaranty,”

The annual rate of interest charged will be between 0% and 12%... The amount being financed will be required to be re-paid in equal monthly installments and the period of repayment will be between 12 months and 60 months

rows.summary: Identical substantive disclosure: both versions state the franchisor offers no financing—directly or indirectly—and provides no guarantees of franchisee obligations.

NEW introduces a materially new, detailed third-party financing program with Sculptor Real Estate Acquisition LP — including co-guaranteed senior/mezzanine loans, specific loan-to-cost ratios (up to 75%), SOFR-based interest rates, exit fees, minimum return multiples, and extensive security/collateral requirements — which is entirely absent from OLD. OLD only describes franchisor-offered deferrals and Development Incentives. NEW also adds two new franchisor-sponsored incentive programs (WOTR and BOLD) with targeted funding amounts and eligibility criteria, and modifies Development Incentive amortization (1/20th or 1/15th annual forgiveness) and security terms ('we do not typically require any additional security... but reserve the right').

oldEvidence.quote: We may offer certain “Development Incentives” for new construction and conversion System HotelsChain Facilities.
The Development Incentive is a loan that is not subject to repayment unless the franchise terminates or a transfer occurs before the end of the term of the Development Incentive Note.
We have no practice or intent to sell, assign or discount to a third party all or part of any financing arrangement above.

newEvidence.quote: Together with Sculptor Real Estate Acquisition LP (together with its affiliates, “Sculptor”), a company that offers real estate financing, we have developed a construction loan program for qualifying franchisees (the “Third Party Financing Program”).

Sculptor is not affiliated with us. The finance program consists of two parts: (1) Sculptor offers senior mortgage loans to qualified franchisees... and our immediate parent, Wyndham Hotel Group, provides Sculptor a partial guaranty...

You will be able to get financing from Sculptor... of up to a 70% of loan-to-cost ratio and additional financing from us of up to a 5% of loan-to-cost ratio; for a total loan-to-cost ratio of up to 75%.

rows.summary: Identical substantive text; only page number and footer number differ (22 → 23). No change in financing offer, terms, or disclosures.
Identical text stating ZIPS does not offer direct or indirect financing and does not guarantee notes, leases, or obligations; only pagination differs.
